metadata.title: John Moya Cantillana | Full Stack Developer
metadata.description: Full Stack Developer specialized in Next.js, React and TypeScript. Builder of scalable web platforms with over 32,000 users.

hero.name: John Moya Cantillana
hero.title: Full Stack Developer
hero.description: I design, build, and deploy production-ready web applications with a strong focus on architecture, scalability, and product needs.

about.title: About Me
about.description: Full Stack Developer with experience building complex web platforms from scratch. My approach combines solid software architecture with practical product feature implementation.

fandoms.name: Fandoms.io
fandoms.description: Designed and built a complete social platform for fan communities, taking it from concept to production with thousands of active users.

caseStudy.title: Case Study
caseStudy.subtitle: Complete social platform for fan communities • 32,000+ active users

overview.title: Project Overview
overview.description: Social platform for fan communities, developed from scratch as an individual project. Designed to support high-volume content, frequent interactions, and scalable performance.

role.title: My Role
role.description: I was responsible for the full development lifecycle of the project, including architecture design, frontend and backend implementation, authentication, data modeling, and deployment.

problem.title: The Problem
problem.description: The main challenge was designing a platform capable of handling dynamic content, user-generated interactions, and SEO-heavy pages while remaining maintainable and secure. Fan communities needed a Spanish-language solution that combined Reddit's structure with Discord's real-time interaction.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: The platform is built with Next.js 15 (App Router) and React 19, with a modular structure that separates UI (src/components), business logic/state (src/hooks, src/contexts, src/store) and data access/services (src/lib).
architecture.backend: The data backend relies on Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + RPC), using Row Level Security (RLS) to enforce permissions at the database level and reduce permission logic in the app.
architecture.rendering: For rendering, the project uses a hybrid approach: public/SEO pages leverage Server Components + SSG/ISR (revalidate/metadata), while highly interactive flows (feed, messages, moderation) are implemented with Client Components consuming Supabase from the browser when applicable.
architecture.media: For media, Cloudflare R2 is used with presigned URLs (generated by Route Handlers in Node.js) and direct upload from client to R2; the app stores URLs in Postgres and handles deletions as needed.

solution.title: Core Features
solution.description: A unified platform with community system, moderation, interactive content, real-time chat and gamification.

features.communities: Community system with hierarchical roles and role-based access control
features.content: Community-based content system with posts, comments and voting
features.moderation: Complete moderation panel with database-level policies
features.polls: Interactive polling system with support for classic and multi-vote modes
features.badges: Badge and achievement system with gamification
features.realtime: Real-time chat per community and direct messaging via Supabase Realtime

nextjs.title: Next.js 15 + React 19
nextjs.reason: Chosen to leverage App Router, Server Components and the latest React 19 optimizations, keeping the application scalable with better DX.

supabase.title: Supabase (PostgreSQL + RLS)
supabase.reason: Selected to reduce backend overhead, leveraging PostgreSQL with Row Level Security to enforce permissions at the database level.

serverComponents.title: Hybrid Rendering
serverComponents.reason: Server Components + SSG/ISR for public/SEO pages, Client Components for interactive flows (feed, messages, moderation).

seo.title: SEO-First Design
seo.reason: SEO considerations were incorporated early, using revalidate and metadata for indexable dynamic pages.

auth.title: Robust PWA Authentication
auth.problem: OAuth authentication and session handling had complex edge cases in PWA environments.
auth.solution: Hybrid session persistence system with fallbacks, multi-session conflict detection and adaptive timeouts.

realtime.title: Scalable Real-time
realtime.problem: Maintaining efficient Realtime connections without degrading performance.
realtime.solution: Reusable broadcast channel pattern with reference counting and automatic cleanup.

feed.title: Performant Feed
feed.problem: Loading feeds with complex data without N+1 queries.
feed.solution: Efficient pagination with infinite scroll, optimized queries with selective JOINs and TanStack Query cache.

roles.title: Granular Permission System
roles.problem: Different permissions based on context (admin, moderator, author).
roles.solution: Row Level Security with composite PostgreSQL policies and dual client-server verification.

tradeOffs.title: Trade-offs & Future Improvements
tradeOffs.description: While the current architecture supports the project's goals, future improvements could include:
improvements: Background jobs for heavy processing tasks
Additional caching layers for high-traffic endpoints
Further optimization of database queries as usage grows
Full internationalization to expand to other markets

learnings.title: Key Learnings
learnings.description: Technical and product lessons learned during platform development.
items: Initial architecture matters: refactoring tables with data is complex
RLS is powerful but requires careful planning
Aggressive caching improves UX but complicates invalidation
PWA authentication edge cases require exhaustive testing
Real-time metrics help make product decisions

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Interested in working together?
contact.description: I'm looking for opportunities where I can contribute solid technical solutions while continuing to learn from more experienced developers.
